items.title: P0: Implement web_search tool
items.content:
## Problem
No `web_search` tool exists. Agents cannot search the web.

## Requirements
- `web_search` tool using DuckDuckGo (no API key needed)
- Returns titles, URLs, and snippets
- Parameters: query (required), count (1-10), region (optional)

## Reference
OpenClaw uses DuckDuckGo HTML scraping. Same approach works.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agent can search the web and get results
- Results include title, URL, snippet
- No API key required
items.author: steins-z

items.title: P0: Implement read tool
items.content:
## Problem
No `read` tool exists. This is the most fundamental file tool — agents cannot read file contents without it.

## Requirements
- `read` tool: read file contents with optional offset/limit for large files
- Support text files (return content as string)
- Support images (return as base64 or attachment depending on provider)
- Respect workspace-only guards (`allowedWorkspaces`)
- Truncation for very large files (e.g., 50KB or 2000 lines)

## Reference
OpenClaw's read tool signature:
```
read(path, offset?, limit?)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agent can read any file within allowed workspaces
- Large files are truncated with guidance to use offset/limit
- Files outside workspace are rejected when workspaceOnly is true
- Tests cover: normal read, offset/limit, workspace guard, image files, non-existent file
items.author: steins-z

items.title: fix: [Bug]: tools.exec.security: full does not enable inline Python execution (strictInlineEval still blocks)
items.content:
## Summary

Disable strict inline-eval policy requirements when security mode is set to full. The strictInlineEval option will now only block execution when running in standard (allowlist) or untrusted mode.

## Changes

- Update strictInlineEvalRequiresApproval in `invoke-system-run.ts` to require security !== "full"

## Testing

Updated tests to verify that strict inline eval tests fail in allowlist mode but pass in full mode.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#65102
items.author: mr-kelly

items.title: Task state not reconciled on gateway restart — stale running tasks accumulate
items.content:
### Problem

When a gateway restart or `/reset` kills running sub-agent processes, their task records remain in `status=running` indefinitely. Over time these stale records accumulate, inflating the running count and polluting audit results.

### Reproduction

1. Spawn sub-agents (e.g. via `sessions_spawn`)
2. While sub-agents are running, restart gateway or `/reset` the session
3. Run `openclaw tasks audit` — stale_running and lost tasks appear
4. Run `openclaw tasks maintenance --apply` — 0 tasks reconciled (no-op)

### Current Behavior

- `openclaw tasks audit` correctly detects stale_running tasks and lost task_runs
- `openclaw tasks maintenance` does NOT auto-reconcile them (reports "0 reconcile")
- No crash recovery happens on gateway startup — stale records persist until manual DB surgery

### Expected Behavior

On gateway startup, automatically reconcile all `status=running` tasks whose sub-agent processes no longer exist → mark as `lost` or `timed_out`. This is basic crash recovery for any task orchestration system.

Alternatively, `openclaw tasks maintenance --apply` should handle these cases.

### Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.4.9 (0512059)
- Node v22.22.0
- Linux 5.15.0-113-generic (x64)

items.title: [codex] adopt v2026.4.11 manifest descriptors
items.content:
## Summary
- bump the local `openclaw` dev pin to `2026.4.11` while keeping the external plugin compatibility floor at `>=2026.4.9`
- add `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest `activation` and `setup` descriptors for `openclaw-code-agent`
- add repo guardrails and docs so the descriptor metadata stays aligned with the plugin's real surface

## What Changed
- `package.json` / `pnpm-lock.yaml`
  - refreshed the local development host pin to `openclaw@2026.4.11`
- `openclaw.plugin.json`
  - added `activation.onCommands` for the plugin-owned chat commands:
    - `agent`
    - `agent_kill`
    - `agent_output`
    - `agent_respond`
    - `agent_sessions`
    - `agent_stats`
    - `goal`
    - `goal_status`
    - `goal_stop`
  - added `activation.onCapabilities: ["tool"]`
  - added a minimal `setup` descriptor with `requiresRuntime: false`
- `tests/plugin-entry.test.ts`
  - now asserts both the unchanged external compatibility floor and the new `2026.4.11` local dev pin
  - now asserts the manifest activation/setup descriptor shape directly
- `README.md` and `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md`
  - document how the new manifest descriptors are intended to work and what they deliberately do not claim yet

## Activation / Setup Behavior
- activation is intentionally narrow and manifest-first
  - the plugin advertises the chat commands it owns plus its tool capability
  - this gives `v2026.4.11` a cheap discovery/activation hint without having to infer ownership from the runtime at load time
- setup is intentionally minimal
  - `requiresRuntime: false` tells the host there is no setup-only runtime handoff needed after descriptor lookup
  - no provider, CLI-backend, or config-migration descriptors are advertised because this plugin does not currently expose those setup-time surfaces

## Caveats
- this does **not** add provider onboarding metadata or setup-runtime code; that would be overclaiming the plugin's current setup surface
- the public compatibility floor remains `>=2026.4.9`; this PR only
items.author: goldmar

items.title: Antenna skill: incorrect capability tags (`requires-wallet`, `requires-oauth-token`) and questionable VirusTotal "suspicious" verdict
items.content:
## Summary

The [Antenna](https://clawhub.ai/cshirley001/antenna) skill (v1.2.7) is flagged as **suspicious** on ClawHub with three capability tags: `Crypto`, `Requires wallet`, and `Requires OAuth token`. Two of these tags are factually incorrect, and the VirusTotal verdict appears to conflate legitimate security architecture with malicious behavior.

## Incorrect capability tags

### `Requires wallet` — false positive

Antenna has **no wallet functionality** of any kind. It does not:
- interact with cryptocurrency wallets
- store, send, or receive funds
- reference any blockchain, Web3, or wallet API
- contain any code path resembling wallet operations

The likely trigger is Antenna's use of `age` encryption for peer bootstrap exchange and `openssl rand -hex` for secret generation. These are standard cryptographic operations for peer authentication, not wallet behavior.

### `Requires OAuth token` — false positive

Antenna does **not** use OAuth. Its authentication model is:
- **Webhook bearer tokens** — static shared secrets stored in local files (`chmod 600`), passed via `Authorization: Bearer` headers to OpenClaw's built-in `/hooks/agent` endpoint
- **Per-peer identity secrets** — hex strings for sender verification

There is no OAuth flow, no token refresh, no authorization server, no client ID/secret, no redirect URI, no scope negotiation — none of the defining characteristics of OAuth. The scanner appears to have pattern-matched on `Authorization: Bearer` and `token` references without distinguishing webhook auth from OAuth.

### `Crypto` — accurate but misleading in context

Antenna does use cryptographic operations:
- `age` encryption for bootstrap exchange bundles
- `openssl rand` for secret generation
- Bearer token authentication

This tag is factually correct but contributes to a misleading overall impression when combined with the two false tags above.

## VirusTotal "suspicious" verdict

The VT analysis acknowledges Antenna is "clearly aligned with

items.title: Add `--targets` option for internal network scanning with CIDR support
items.content:
### Motivation

- 支持针对内网 IP/网段主动探测 OpenClaw 网关与未授权访问风险以补充本地检测能力。 
- 为命令行增加可指定扫描目标的能力，便于在托管或企业网络中批量检测暴露实例。 
- 通过并发探测与简单 HTTP 探针快速识别端口开放与认证缺失场景以便及时告警。

### Description

- 在 CLI 中新增 `--targets` 参数并在 `internal/app/app.go` 中解析并传递到扫描器。 
- 增加 `internal/scanner/targets.go` 实现 `ParseTargets`，支持逗号分隔 IP 与 CIDR 展开并排除网络/广播地址。 
- 增加 `internal/scanner/network_targets.go` 实现 `ScanTargetNetwork`，使用并发 TCP 探测与 HTTP 探针识别开放端口与未授权访问风险。 
- 将远程目标扫描接入现有 `network` 检查中（`internal/scanner/scanner.go`），并更新文档 `README.md` 与 `docs/usage.md` 展示新选项。

### Testing

- 运行了单元测试套件 `go test ./...` 以验证新增与现有功能的兼容性。 
- 包含并通过的新增测试包括 `TestParseTargetsIPAndCIDR`, `TestParseTargetsRejectsInvalid`, `TestScanTargetNetworkDetectsOpenPort`, `TestScanTargetNetworkDetectsAuthRisk` 与 `TestParseOptionsParsesTargets`. 
- 所有自动化测试均已通过且未见失败。

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_69d8bd4279e8832eb8839c7d318e7e48)

items.title: Control UI cron display: `*/6` intervals shown as `3:00` instead of "every 6 hours"
items.content:
## Bug Description
In the Control UI → Automation → Cron Jobs panel, the "Memory Dreaming - Light" cron job shows "3:00" for all three phases, but the actual schedule expressions are:

- **Light**: `0 */6 * * *` (every 6 hours — e.g., 0:00, 6:00, 12:00, 18:00)
- **Deep**: `0 3 * * *` (daily 3:00 AM)
- **REM**: `0 5 * * 0` (weekly Sunday 5:00 AM)

Gateway logs confirm the correct expressions are stored and executed:
```
[plugins] memory-core-dreaming: created "Memory Dreaming - Light" (0 */6 * * * @ Asia/Shanghai)
[plugins] memory-core-dreaming: created "Memory Dreaming - Deep" (0 3 * * * @ Asia/Shanghai)
[plugins] memory-core-dreaming: created "Memory Dreaming - REM" (0 5 * * 0 @ Asia/Shanghai)
```

The display bug is in the Control UI cron expression renderer — it appears to treat `*/6` as a fixed minute value (00) at hour 3, instead of "every 6 hours."

## Environment
- OpenClaw: 2026.4.11
- Node: macOS
- Control UI: web-based dashboard

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: "Every 6 hours" or display the raw expression `0 */6 * * *`
- Actual: "3:00"

## Reproduction
1. Install memory-core-dreaming plugin
2. Observe cron jobs in Control UI → Automation → Cron Jobs
3. All three Dreaming phases show "3:00" regardless of their actual schedule

items.title: chore(publish): publish helm chart to artifact hub
items.content:
## Merge Contract

Pull request titles must be conventional commit messages and must not use any registered tool name.
Treat `codex` as a reserved tool name too; do not use it in branch names or pull request titles.
Do not open or update a pull request until every changed executable source file is covered 100% by automated unit tests.

### Behavioral Change

- Extend the Helm publish workflow so successful OCI chart publishes also register the `oci://ghcr.io/<owner>/charts/devplat` repository in Artifact Hub using `HELM_KEY_ID` and `HELM_KEY_SECRET`.
- Push an `artifacthub-repo.yml` metadata artifact to the OCI chart repository with ORAS so Artifact Hub can resolve repository metadata from the published chart location.

### Risk

- Artifact Hub publication now depends on valid `HELM_KEY_ID` and `HELM_KEY_SECRET` repository secrets and on those API credentials having permission to upsert the target repository.

### Schema and Artifact Impact

- Adds the Artifact Hub OCI metadata artifact tagged as `artifacthub.io` to the published Helm chart repository in GHCR.

### GitHub / Discord / OpenClaw / Operator Impact

- GitHub Actions now manages Artifact Hub Helm repository registration and metadata publication as part of the Helm release path.

### Performance Impact

- Negligible. The workflow adds a small number of API and OCI metadata operations after chart publication.

### Release Impact

- Published Helm charts are now registered with Artifact Hub and ship Artifact Hub repository metadata alongside the OCI chart artifacts.

### Rollback Notes

- Revert this PR to remove the Artifact Hub registration and metadata publication steps from the Helm workflow.

## Validation Performed

- `npm run check:pre-push`

### Validation Checklist

- [x] `npm run check:repo`
- [x] `npm run check:changed-coverage`
- [x] `npm run test:coverage`
- [x] `npm run docs:build`

items.title: [DingTalk] Make ACK reaction configurable
items.content:
## Background

When the bot receives a message, it adds a persistent "收到" reaction via
`POST /v1.0/robot/emotion/reply` as a delivery acknowledgment. The reaction
type is currently hardcoded in `__init__` as:

- `emotionName` / `text`: `"收到"`
- `emotionId`: `"2659900"`
- `backgroundId`: `"im_bg_1"`

The config key `ack_reaction` already exists but only supports two values:
`"emoji"` (enable, always "收到") and `"none"` (disable entirely).

## Feature Request

Extend `ack_reaction` to accept a reaction name so users can choose which
emoji/sticker appears as the ACK. The existing `"none"` value for disabling
should continue to work as-is.

Example `config.yaml`:

```yaml
platforms:
  dingtalk:
    extra:
      ack_reaction: "收到"      # default — current behavior
      # ack_reaction: "OK"      # alternative
      # ack_reaction: "none"    # disable entirely
```

## Implementation Notes

- The payload is built once at `__init__` time (lines ~150–159 of
  `dingtalk.py`). A lookup table mapping reaction names to their
  `emotionId` / `backgroundId` values is the natural approach.
- DingTalk's `/robot/emotion/reply` API accepts a fixed set of reactions;
  the lookup table should document which names are valid, and the adapter
  should warn (non-fatal) when an unknown name is configured and fall back
  to `"收到"`.
- The DingTalk open-platform docs list available emotion IDs; the reference
  TypeScript implementation (`../openclaw-channel-dingtalk/`) may have a
  mapping worth reusing.

items.author: tangyuan-dev

items.title: [Browser][Docker][macOS arm64] Managed profile starts Chrome but fails CDP ready check; manual launch succeeds
items.content:
## Summary

On macOS Docker Desktop (Apple Silicon), a pure-container OpenClaw deployment can run the gateway and browser service, but the **managed local browser profile** fails with:

`Chrome CDP websocket for profile "openclaw" is not reachable after start.`

The key detail is that **the same Chromium binary, the same profile directory, and effectively the same launch flags work when started manually inside the container**. This points to a bug in OpenClaw's managed-launch / CDP-ready path, not a generic Chromium-in-container failure.

## Environment

- OpenClaw image: `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest`
- Actual base digest: `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw@sha256:dd11fba81ea21ba405c2ca8479610f968e5d3a56455ed173c434aff15fbd615f`
- Runtime version reported by gateway/CLI: `2026.4.11`
- Host OS: macOS 26.4 / Darwin 25.4.0 / arm64
- Container runtime: Docker Desktop 4.68.0, Docker Engine 29.3.1, Compose v5.1.1
- Deployment mode: pure-container gateway + CLI, workspace bind-mounted from host
- Browser binary: Playwright Chromium at `/home/node/.cache/ms-playwright/chromium-1217/chrome-linux/chrome`
- Browser config used:

```json
{
  "browser": {
    "executablePath": "/home/node/.cache/ms-playwright/chromium-1217/chrome-linux/chrome",
    "headless": true,
    "noSandbox": true,
    "extraArgs": [
      "--disable-dev-shm-usage",
      "--remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1"
    ]
  }
}
```

## Reproduction

1. Run OpenClaw gateway in Docker on macOS arm64.
2. Preinstall Playwright Chromium in the container.
3. Set `browser.executablePath` to the Playwright Chromium binary.
4. Set `browser.noSandbox=true` and restart the gateway.
5. Run:

```bash
openclaw browser start
```

## Actual behavior

Gateway log shows Chrome starting, then later failing the CDP readiness check:

```text
[browser/chrome] 🦞 openclaw browser started (custom) profile "openclaw" on 127.0.0.1:18800 (pid 345)
[gateway/ws] ⇄ res ✗ browser.request ... errorMessage=Error: Chrome CDP websocket for profile "

## Summary
- **Split-panel layout**: Agent list (left) with collapsible machines/configs sections + full detail view (right)

## Architecture

When you're ready to do a release, you can merge this and publish to npm yourself or [setup this action to publish automatically](https://github.com/changesets/action#with-publishing). If you're not ready to do a release yet, that's fine, whenever you add more changesets to main, this PR will be updated.


# Releases
## @martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.9.0

### Minor Changes

-   [#355](https://github.com/Martian-Engineering/lossless-claw/pull/355) [`6e9388c`](https://github.com/Martian-Engineering/lossless-claw/commit/6e9388c17036caa6021ab075e4d91ee928d73986) Thanks [@LanicBlue](https://github.com/LanicBlue)! - Externalize inline base64 images before large tool-result text compaction, and add `largeFilesDir` / `LCM_LARGE_FILES_DIR` so externalized payload storage can be configured explicitly.

### Patch Changes

-   [#403](https://github.com/Martian-Engineering/lossless-claw/pull/403) [`ea7d532`](https://github.com/Martian-Engineering/lossless-claw/commit/ea7d5327d648790350724c15990b5c1ab98bf611) Thanks [@jetd1](https://github.com/jetd1)! - Convert bootstrap's file I/O off the Node.js event loop. `readFileSegment` and `readLastJsonlEntryBeforeOffset` previously used sync `openSync`/`readSync`/`statSync`, which could block the gateway for minutes while scanning multi-MB JSONL transcripts during the bootstrap append-only path. The bootstrap entry `statSync` and `refreshBootstrapState` helper are now async as well. The backward-scan loop now only reads new chunks when the current carry has no more newlines, and the fast path short-circuits before the backward scan when the DB's latest hash no longer matches the checkpoint (the common case during active sessions, where the scan can never succeed).

items.author: neo44hd

items.title: fix: resolve all CI test failures — thread leaks, retry hangs, info leak, re-enable pytest
items.content:
Three independent root causes were causing the CI test suite to hang and fail. `pytest` was also fully bypassed in `ci.yml` as a workaround.

## Root causes & fixes

### 1. aiosqlite thread leak on corrupt DB (`registry.py`, `async_registry.py`)
`PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL` was called **before** the `try/except` block. On a non-SQLite file it raises `DatabaseError`, which propagated uncaught — leaving the aiosqlite background thread alive. At pytest shutdown, Python waited on the non-daemon thread indefinitely.

```python
# Before — PRAGMA outside try block, connection never closed on error
self._conn = await aiosqlite.connect(self._db_path)
await self._conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")   # DatabaseError leaks thread
try:
    ...
except Exception:
    ...

# After — everything inside try, connection always closed on any exception
try:
    await self._conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
    ...
except Exception:
    await self._conn.close()
    self._conn = None
    raise
```

### 2. Retry backoff too slow for tests (`nexus_downloader.py`, `sync_engine.py`)
`@retry(wait=wait_exponential(min=5, max=120), stop=stop_after_attempt(5))` meant hash-mismatch and network-failure tests each waited **~75 seconds** before failing. Replaced class-level `@retry` decorators with inline `AsyncRetrying` and a constructor-injected wait strategy:

```python
# Production default unchanged; tests inject wait_none()
NexusDownloader(..., download_retry_wait=wait_none())
SyncEngine(..., fetch_retry_wait=wait_none())
```

All test helpers (`_make_downloader`, `SyncEngine` fixtures) now pass `wait_none()`.

### 3. Exception detail leaked in `/api/chat` 500 response (`app.py`)
`str(exc)` was returned directly to the client. Replaced with a static generic message; exception is still logged server-side. Updated the contradictory test in `test_web.py` that was asserting the opposite (exception text present in response).

## CI
Re-enabled `pytest` in `ci.yml` — **851 tests pass, 2 skipped*

items.title: Create BIbliotec — Python digital religious library with AI integration
items.content:
Implements a complete Python application: a styled digital religious library with local AI Q&A, built from an empty repository.

## Core modules (`src/`)
- **`interface.py`** — 1080-line CustomTkinter GUI with sepia/parchment book theme; 3 tabs: Library reader, Search, AI chat
- **`pesquisa.py`** — Search engine supporting keyword queries and direct references (`Gênesis 1:1`, `Sura 2:255`); fan-out across all books simultaneously
- **`ia_manager.py`** — Ollama + Open WebUI integration with async streaming; auto-injects relevant verse context into prompts
- **`leitor.py`** — Cached chapter/verse reader with full book-tree navigation
- **`utils.py`** — Config loading, book discovery, path resolution

## Sacred texts (`livros/`)
Eight JSON files with representative content in pt-BR:

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `biblia.json` | Genesis 1–2, Psalm 23, John 1 |
| `tora.json` | 5 books of Moses (samples) |
| `talmud.json` | Berakhot, Shabbat, Sanhedrin mishnahs |
| `alcorao.json` | Al-Fatiha, Al-Baqara 1–20, Al-Ikhlas |
| `bhagavad_gita.json` | Chapters 1–2 |
| `tao_te_ching.json` | All 81 chapters (complete) |
| `dhammapada.json` | Yamakavagga + Appamadavagga |
| `livro_de_mormon.json` | 1 Nephi 1, Moroni 10 |

## Tooling
- **`baixar_livros.py`** — Downloads full Quran via `api.alquran.cloud/v1/quran/pt.ElHayek`; stubs for Bible API expansion
- **`iniciar.bat`** — Windows launcher: checks Python, creates venv, installs deps, runs `baixar_livros.py` if needed, starts app
- **`config.json`** — Configures window size, Ollama URL (`localhost:11434`), Open WebUI URL (`localhost:3000`), default model, search limits
- **`requirements.txt`** — `customtkinter`, `requests`, `Pillow`

items.title: feat(ee06): compaction gate and findings surfacing
items.content:
## Summary

- `advance` no longer auto-starts the next ticket. It emits `compaction_required=true` and a directive to call `/compact`, then stops. The `--no-start-next` flag is removed.
- `start` (zero-arg) finds the next pending ticket, creates its worktree and handoff, and prints the handoff path. Explicit `start <ticket-id>` form unchanged.
- `formatCurrentTicketStatus` emits a condensed findings block when actionable `reviewComments` exist: `[vendor] path:line — title` per finding. Title extracted via first `**...**` bold match, 120-char fallback. Outdated and resolved findings suppressed.

Addresses the two EE5 shortcomings identified in the Phase 15 execution retrospective. See `docs/03-engineering/epic-06-compaction-gate-and-findings-surfacing.md`.

## Test plan

- [ ] `advanceToNextTicket` marks reviewed ticket done without starting next (new test)
- [ ] `advanceToNextTicket` throws when no reviewed ticket is ready (new test)
- [ ] `formatCurrentTicketStatus` emits findings block for actionable findings (new test)
- [ ] `formatCurrentTicketStatus` suppresses outdated/resolved findings (new test)
- [ ] `formatCurrentTicketStatus` omits block when no comments (new test)
- [ ] `formatCurrentTicketStatus` falls back to truncated body when no bold title (new test)
- [ ] Full verify clean (`bun run verify:quiet`)

items.title: research: harness architecture RFC -- middleware + contracts + brain/hands/session (+ evolution path)
items.content:
> **Research candidate -- RFC required before any code lands**. This issue tracks an architectural direction informed by multiple external sources. No implementation or adoption decision has been made. Output of this issue is a design RFC, not code.

## Sources

- **LangChain Middleware blog**: [How middleware lets you customize your agent harness](https://blog.langchain.com/how-middleware-lets-you-customize-your-agent-harness/)
- **Martin Fowler**: [Harness Engineering](https://martinfowler.com/articles/harness-engineering.html)
- **Anthropic Engineering**: [Managed Agents -- brain / hands / session decoupling](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/managed-agents)
- **Paper (harness externalization)**: [NLAH: Natural-Language Agent Harnesses (arxiv 2603.25723)](https://huggingface.co/papers/2603.25723)
- **LangChain Better Harness**: [A recipe for harness hill-climbing with evals](https://blog.langchain.com/better-harness-a-recipe-for-harness-hill-climbing-with-evals/)

## Core idea

The five sources converge on the same architectural question from different angles: **what does a well-designed agent harness look like, and can it evolve?**

1. **Middleware pattern (LangChain)** -- composable hooks (`before_agent` / `after_agent` / `before_model` / `after_model` / `wrap_model_call` / `wrap_tool_call`) for PII redaction, retry, context management, dynamic tool selection, without monolithic controller code.
2. **Feedforward + feedback controls (Fowler)** -- harness engineering is the sum of *feedforward* (documentation, standards, prompt guidance) and *feedback* (tests, linters, review agents, sensors). Both are required.
3. **Brain / hands / session decoupling (Anthropic)** -- LLM harness (brain), sandboxes/tools (hands), and session event log are independently provisioned for resilience, credential isolation, and latency improvements.
4. **Harness externalization (NLAH)** -- portable executable harnesses expressed as editable natural-language artifacts with runtime c

items.title: [Tracking] hermes claw migrate — known bugs, schema drift, and gaps
items.content:
## Context

`hermes claw migrate` handles persona, memory, user profile, skills, model config, TTS, MCP servers, command allowlists, and messaging platform settings across 30+ categories. The two-layer design (CLI wrapper in `hermes_cli/claw.py` + migration engine in `openclaw_to_hermes.py`) is thorough for the config landscape it was written against.

The problem is that landscape has shifted. OpenClaw has restructured workspace paths, channel config schemas, TTS provider names, provider API type identifiers, and the `thinkingDefault` enum. The migrate tool silently finds nothing at the old locations, skipping data without warning. This is the single most discussed pain point in the community (~180 messages from ~60 users in a two-week window).

This issue tracks all known problems. Each claim has been verified against the current source code and cross-referenced against the current OpenClaw config schema.

---

## PRs Filed

| # | PR | What | Priority | Lines | Status |
|---|-----|------|----------|-------|--------|
| 1 | #7869 | Find files in `workspace-main/` (memories/persona fix) | **P0** | +13 | Open |
| 2 | #7860 | Read API keys from `openclaw.json["env"]` sub-object | **P0** | +15 | Open |
| 3 | #5207 | Copy `openclaw.json` post-migration (pre-existing) | **P0** | ~16 | Open |
| 4 | #7861 | Extract channel tokens from `accounts.default.*` paths | P1 | +31/-20 | Open |
| 5 | #7864 | Warn about SecretRefs, skills reload, WhatsApp re-pair | P1 | +37 | Open |
| 6 | #7868 | Provider api types, thinkingDefault enum, Matrix `accessToken` | P1 | +14/-8 | Open |
| 7 | #7862 | Handle TTS `edge` -> `microsoft` rename | P2 | +6/-5 | Open |

All independent, all single-file (`openclaw_to_hermes.py`), all against `main`.

| #5639 | Agent profile migration docs | Open |

items.author: SHL0MS

items.title: Investigate Sunaku's home row mods refinements (tap-preferred, per-finger timing, streak decay)
items.content:
## Background

Sunaku publishes an influential home row mods methodology at https://sunaku.github.io/home-row-mods.html that refines the ZMK hold-tap approach with a few non-obvious tricks. This keyboard already uses home row mods (`hml`/`hmr` on D/F/G and H/J/K — see `config/eyelash_sofle.keymap:44-68`), so this issue is about deciding which of Sunaku's refinements are worth adopting, not introducing HRM from scratch.

## Current state (reference)

| Setting | Current (`hml`/`hmr`/`ralt_mt`) |
|---|---|
| `flavor` | `balanced` |
| `tapping-term-ms` | 280 |
| `require-prior-idle-ms` | 150 |
| `quick-tap-ms` | 175 |
| `hold-trigger-key-positions` | opposite hand + thumbs |
| `hold-trigger-on-release` | yes |
| Per-finger tuning | none (identical for all fingers) |

## Sunaku's approach — core ideas

1. **`tap-preferred` flavor** over `balanced`. He reports crisper typing latency at the cost of slightly more disambiguation risk (which he claws back via streak suppression).
2. **Aggressive `require-prior-idle-ms` (~230 ms)** to suppress HRM during typing streaks — prevents \"clock\"-style same-hand rolls from chording mods.
3. **Per-finger timing**, grouped by dexterity:
   - Pinky / ring / middle: long streak decay (~230 ms), longer tapping-term.
   - **Index: much shorter streak decay (~70 ms)** because the index finger both rolls and holds unpredictably; long decay makes CamelCase Shift unusable.
4. **Shift exempted from streak suppression** so sentence-end punctuation (`!`, `?`) works mid-typing-streak.
5. **Bigger `quick-tap-ms` (~300 ms)** for auto-repeat ergonomics.
6. **\"Sandwich\" with hand-specific layers**: first hold jumps to a layer that masks same-side keys with additional hold-taps, giving a second line of defense beyond `hold-trigger-key-positions`. Probably overkill for this board — our positional restriction already blocks same-hand activation entirely.

## Relevance to this keyboard

| Refinement | Likely value here | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `tap-pr
items.author: rdlu

items.title: feat: add /lcm rotate and /lcm backup commands
items.content:
## Summary

Closes #394.

This adds two small plugin commands aimed at the large-session operational gap in lossless-claw:

- `/lcm backup` / `/lossless backup`
- `/lcm rotate` / `/lossless rotate`

The core user problem is that a long-lived OpenClaw session can accumulate into one very large active LCM conversation row because LCM correctly reuses the row for the stable `sessionKey`.

## Why not `/new` or `/reset`?

Today the existing commands solve adjacent problems, but not this one:

- `/new` prunes context, but keeps writing into the same active LCM row
- `/reset` changes OpenClaw session flow, which is heavier than users often want when the real issue is just LCM row size

This patch adds the missing middle ground: keep the live OpenClaw session identity, but split LCM storage.

## What changed

- adds `/lcm backup` and `/lossless backup` to create a timestamped SQLite snapshot on demand
- adds `/lcm rotate` and `/lossless rotate` to archive the current active LCM row and start a fresh row for the same live session
- reuses shared backup plumbing rather than inventing a second backup path
- fixes bootstrap so a freshly rotated empty conversation with checkpoint state starts **from now forward** instead of re-importing older transcript history
- prefers the active conversation when multiple rows share the same `sessionKey`
- hardens command and engine failure handling so backup / rotate return structured failure output instead of bubbling raw runtime errors
- documents why `/new`, `/reset`, and `/lcm rotate` solve different problems

## Why this is useful now

Cross-session search and retrieval change the tradeoff here in a good way: once archived rows remain searchable, splitting an oversized active row is usually a performance win.

The active row stays smaller and cheaper to bootstrap, assemble, and maintain, while archived history remains available for search and retrieval across conversations.

## Validation

- `npm test` ✅ (`40` files / `699` tests)
- `np

items.title: [Epic 5] Per-user per-skill rate limiting middleware
items.content:
## Summary
Add lightweight per-user, per-skill rate limiting to prevent abuse and respect external API quotas. ADO uses per-user PAT routing (own quota). GitHub has 5,000 req/hour per token. Rate limits are enforced at the MCP client layer before forwarding calls.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `IRateLimiter` interface: `Task<bool> TryAcquireAsync(string userSub, string skillId, CancellationToken ct)` + `RateLimitConfig` per skill
- [ ] `SkillRateLimiter` implementation: sliding window per user per skill, configurable limits from `appsettings.json`
- [ ] Default limits: GitHub 4,500 req/hour (safety margin below 5,000), ADO 10,000 req/hour
- [ ] Rate limiter checked in `McpToolDispatcher` before executing tool call
- [ ] When rate limited: return structured `rate_limited` error with retry-after hint, agent surfaces conversationally
- [ ] Rate limit state stored in-memory (IMemoryCache) — acceptable for single-instance MVP
- [ ] Telemetry: `tool.rate_limited` event logged with skill, user hash, limit hit
- [ ] Unit tests for sliding window logic, limit enforcement, config binding

## Dependencies
- #62 — Tool dispatch (rate limiter is called from dispatcher)

## Out of Scope
- Distributed rate limiting (Redis/etc — post-MVP when scaling beyond single instance)
- AI Foundry TPM budget (separate concern, already tracked in token usage)

items.title: Add complex site/view compilation for issue #45
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes xlab2016/space_db_private#45

This PR implements comprehensive compilation and interpretation for `site` and `view` definitions in the AGI language, as described in the issue.

## What's implemented

### CSS projection support (`css: { ... }`)
- New `CssLanguageParser` parses CSS block literals from AGI `Render()` method returns
- CSS selectors, property declarations, and conditional expressions preserved as-is
- Renders to `<style>` tag injected into the HTML document's `<head>`

### Multi-projection `return` support
- New `ViewRenderResult` class handles `return html: ..., css: { ... };` syntax
- Correctly splits comma-separated projection list (html:, css:, json:, etc.) respecting nested braces
- Both HTML and CSS projections are extracted and combined during rendering

### Typed field and button component parsing
- New `ViewFieldParser` properly parses `field<string>(label: "Username")` extracting generic type and named parameters
- `button<Login_logon>` parsed as `ButtonDefinition` with `ComponentType` reference
- `ViewDefinition.Buttons` changed from `List<string>` to `List<ButtonDefinition>` with optional component type

### `stream<http>` — HttpStreamDevice and HttpDriver
- New `HttpStreamDevice` wraps HTTP client as an AGI stream device
- Supports `get()`, `post()`, `put()`, `delete()`, `config()` method calls
- `HttpDriver` makes real HTTP requests and parses JSON responses
- JSON responses are returned as `Dictionary<string, object?>` with `success` and `status` keys
- `Hal.DefGen` updated to handle `"http"` generalization

### Example: full complex login view
- Added `examples/login_view.agi` with the complete example from issue #45:
  - `Login` view with typed fields, button component (`button<Login_logon>`)
  - Multi-projection `Render()` returning `html: ..., css: { ... }`
  - `Login_logon` button component with constructor, click handler, and its own `Render()`
  - `procedure logon()` using `stream<http>` to call `/api/v1/authentica

items.title: Implement shared cognitive flow with reasoning engine, planning, execution and critique
items.content:
## Resumen

Esta PR consolida el nuevo flujo cognitivo compartido de Máximus sobre el baseline operacional existente.

## Qué agrega

- núcleo cognitivo compartido en `maximus/cognition/`
- `reasoning_engine` como capacidad transversal
- `analyzer`, `planner` y `critic`
- `plan_executor` para ejecución estructurada vía Claw/OpenClaw
- integración con `ollama_client` y `settings`
- `memory_writer`
- `cognition_api`
- restauración/versionado de `orchestrator_api` y `executor_api`
- actualización del `control_loop` para separar:
  - razonamiento puro
  - flujo operacional con análisis → plan → ejecución → crítica

## Qué mejora

- razonamiento puro con IA real para problemas cerrados
- ejecución real vía Claw/MCP para tareas operativas
- verificación de evidencia posterior
- base heredable para futuras APIs y canales
- mejor separación entre cerebro y manos reales

## Validación realizada

- prueba de lógica con respuesta correcta e inferencia indeterminada
- prueba de control real creando y verificando archivos en `/tmp`
- validación por API
- validación por Telegram
- respuesta operativa real sobre Mailcow
items.author: Automatikaenterprice

items.title: feat(tools): native filesystem tools (Read/Write/Edit/MultiEdit/Glob/Grep)
items.content:
## Summary

Adds native `AgentTool` implementations for `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `MultiEdit`, `Glob`, `Grep` as the structured alternative to shell-based file I/O via `exec`. Closes GH #657.

Schemas match Claude Code exactly so LLMs trained on those tools work without adaptation. Twelve commits, every phase of the epic landed:

- **Phase 1** — host-path MVP for all six tools, Claude-exact schemas, `[tools.policy]` wiring (including a fix for the `coding` profile), `fs-tools` cargo feature
- **Correctness fixes** — absolute-path requirement, `ToolRegistry` collision warnings, typed error taxonomy (`not_found` / `permission_denied` / `too_large` / `not_regular_file` / `must_read_before_write` / `path_denied`)
- **Phase 3a** — CRLF-tolerant `Edit` recovery (pairs with Read's `\r` stripping to form a CRLF-transparent pipeline)
- **Phase 3b** — `FsState` per-session read tracker, must-read-before-write invariant, re-read loop detection with `loop_warning` field
- **Phase 4** — full `[tools.fs]` config surface (workspace_root, allow/deny paths, track_reads, must_read_before_write, max_read_bytes, binary_policy, respect_gitignore, checkpoint_before_mutation, context_window_tokens), `FsPathPolicy` with canonicalized enforcement, `FsToolsContext` struct, `docs/src/tools-fs.md` + SUMMARY.md update
- **Phase 4 polish** — opt-in `CheckpointManager` integration, `binary_policy = "base64"` variant, `respect_gitignore` knob
- **Phase 2** — sandbox bridge for Read/Write/Edit/MultiEdit/Glob via shared shell scripts over `Sandbox::exec` (base64 round-trips, typed exit-code error encoding, 512 KB write cap)
- **Phase 2b** — Grep sandbox routing with three output modes
- **Phase 3c** — adaptive Read page size coupled to model context window (`clamp(tokens × 4 × 0.2, 50 KB, 512 KB)`)

Prior art reviewed: `~/code/claw-code`, `~/code/openclaw` (pi-tools.* wrappers — best source for adaptive paging + edit recovery + Claude param compat), `~/code/hermes-agent` (per-task read tracker, path

items.title: fix(chat): restore new-session UI after Nano provider rebase conflict
items.content:
## Summary

- Restores the clean Gemini-style new-session greeting layout (`ProviderSelectionEmptyState`, `ChatMessagesPane`, `ChatInterface`) that was broken by a rebase conflict in #141 (Nano Claude Code provider)
- Re-adds minimal `nano` provider wiring (types, availability map, model config, CLI status check) in `ChatInterface` and `ChatComposer` for compile compatibility with the rest of the codebase
- Nano Claude Code is commented out in the composer provider list per advisor feedback (low usage frequency)

## What happened

PR #141 introduced the Nano Claude Code provider. During the rebase onto main, conflicts in the chat empty-state components were incorrectly resolved — the old verbose provider-selection cards (session mode radio cards, provider grid, model dropdown) replaced the newer streamlined greeting + composer-badge layout. This PR reverts the UI files to their pre-#141 state and layers on only the type-level nano additions needed to keep TypeScript happy.

## Test plan

- [ ] Open a new session — verify "Hi {name} / What should we do next?" greeting with composer badges (Research Session, provider, model, mode) and guided prompt buttons
- [ ] Switch providers in the composer toolbar — all existing providers still work
- [ ] Verify Nano Claude Code backend still functions (the server-side code is unchanged)

items.title: [Paper Note] ClawBench: Can AI Agents Complete Everyday Online Tasks?, Yuxuan Zhang+, arXiv'26, 2026.04
items.content:
# URL
- https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08523
# Authors
  - Yuxuan Zhang
  - Yubo Wang
  - Yipeng Zhu
  - Penghui Du
  - Junwen Miao
  - Xuan Lu
  - Wendong Xu
  - Yunzhuo Hao
  - Songcheng Cai
  - Xiaochen Wang
  - Huaisong Zhang
  - Xian Wu
  - Yi Lu
  - Minyi Lei
  - Kai Zou
  - Huifeng Yin
  - Ping Nie
  - Liang Chen
  - Dongfu Jiang
  - Wenhu Chen
  - Kelsey R. Allen
# Abstract
  - AI agents may be able to automate your inbox, but can they automate other routine aspects of your life? Everyday online tasks offer a realistic yet unsolved testbed for evaluating the next generation of AI agents. To this end, we introduce ClawBench, an evaluation framework of 153 simple tasks that people need to accomplish regularly in their lives and work, spanning 144 live platforms across 15 categories, from completing purchases and booking appointments to submitting job applications. These tasks require demanding capabilities beyond existing benchmarks, such as obtaining relevant information from user-provided documents, navigating multi-step workflows across diverse platforms, and write-heavy operations like filling in many detailed forms correctly. Unlike existing benchmarks that evaluate agents in offline sandboxes with static pages, ClawBench operates on production websites, preserving the full complexity, dynamic nature, and challenges of real-world web interaction. A lightweight interception layer captures and blocks only the final submission request, ensuring safe evaluation without real-world side effects. Our evaluations of 7 frontier models show that both proprietary and open-source models can complete only a small portion of these tasks. For example, Claude Sonnet 4.6 achieves only 33.3%. Progress on ClawBench brings us closer to AI agents that can function as reliable general-purpose assistants.
# Translation (by gpt-5-nano)
- AIエージェントはあなたの受信箱を自動化できるかもしれませんが、生活の他の日常的な側面も自動化できるでしょうか。日々のオンラインタスクは、次世代のAIエージェントを評価するための現実的でありながら未解決のテストベッドを提供します。この目的のために、ClawBenchを提案します。ClawBen

items.title: 📢 财经汇总报告 2026-04-12 07:36
items.content:
# 财经资讯汇总

**最后更新: 2026-04-12 07:35:59**

## 来源: metwarn

[暴雨速報] 天文台在上午11時正發出黃色暴雨警告信號。
表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

位於珠江口的強雷雨區正繼續向東北偏東移動，其強度有所發展，即將開始影響本港。西部地區將首當其衝。讀者應提防大驟雨、猛烈陣風及雷暴。
身在戶外空曠地方的讀者應留意雷暴風險，如有需要應到有遮蔽的地方暫避。

---

本港西南面再有雷雨區正向東北方向移動，預料將在短期內影響市區及港島一帶。讀者應繼續提防大驟雨及雷暴。
身處該區的讀者應持續留意天氣變化，如有需要應到有遮蔽的地方暫避。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午1時40分取消黃色暴雨警告信號。
雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

---

位於香港以南海域的強雷雨區正逐漸向東北移近本港，未來數小時本港部分地區可能受大驟雨、雷暴影響，南部地區將會首當其衝。
身在戶外空曠地方的讀者應留意天氣變化。

---

過去數小時，珠江口一帶海域持續有強雷雨區發展。未來一兩小時香港南部及市區部分地區或再受大驟雨、強陣風及雷暴影響。讀者應提高警覺。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午2時05分發出黃色暴雨警告信號。
表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午5時40分取消黃色暴雨警告信號。
雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

---

中學文憑試筆試將於明日（星期三）開考，主科部分則於星期四開始，MET WARN預先祝福各位考生順順利利，考獲自己心目中的理想成績！
再次提醒各位考生，根據現行準則，即使天文台發出紅色暴雨警告，只要考評局未有宣佈，當日所有考試將如常舉行。考評局相關指引同時提及，若紅色暴雨警告於考試時間前生效，試場主任可彈性處理開考時間，一般會先延遲約15分鐘開考，並可因應需要再延遲15分鐘。
按照現時預測，未來九日本港出現惡劣或不穩定天氣的機會不大。惟明日受到偏東氣流影響，或會有一兩陣驟雨。無論如何，正所謂天有不測之風雲，各位考生出門前都應該留意天氣和交通狀況，免失預算。

---

隨著太平洋高壓增強，並在未來數日支配本港，預料炎熱及晴朗的天氣會持續一段時間。
有異於一般四月偶有東北季候風南下，並夾雜天陰、下雨的天氣，今年四月本港可謂「提早入夏」。從天文台氣溫概率預報亦可見，未來十數日大部分時間，本港最高氣溫均較有可能高於氣候平均。
另一方面，西北太平洋有一個新的熱帶氣旋發展。雖然森拉克吸引了部分媒體的目光，但預料風暴不會進入南海，亦不會直接影響本港天氣。

---

## 来源: kingkitay

【4连板华远控股：本次重组可能涉及控股股东华远集团 不涉及本公司的重大资产重组事项】
华远控股(
600743.SH
)公告称，公司股票连续3个交易日收盘价格涨幅偏离值累计达20%，4月10日继续涨停，连续4个交易日累计涨幅达46.41%。公司于4月10日披露《关于公司实际控制人筹划重组的提示性公告》。公司控股股东北京市华远集团有限公司（简称“华远集团”）来函告知，本公司实际控制人北京市西城区国资委拟对下属部分一级企业集团进行重组（简称“本次重组”），本次重组可能涉及本公司控股股东华远集团，不涉及本公司的重大资产重组事项，不会导致本公司实际控制人发生变更。截至本公告披露日，相关事项仍处于筹划阶段，尚存在不确定性。

---

美联储戴利描述了降息或按兵不动的几种可能情况，并表示加息的可能性不大。
【美联储戴利：将通胀率降至2%至关重要】
美联储戴利表示，将通胀率降至2%至关重要，高通胀数据不会让任何人感到意外。

---

交易员上调对美联储年内降息一次的押注。

---

国内商品期货夜盘开盘涨跌不一，合成橡胶跌3.7%，乙二醇跌超2%，甲醇跌1.87%，对二甲苯跌1.83%，燃油跌1.56%，沪铜涨0.93%，焦煤涨0.66%，纯碱涨0.61%。

---

items.content:
## 📋 skill基本信息

**GitHub仓库**（必填，SKILL.md所在的路径）: 

**支持平台**（必填，若skill只支持部分平台如claudecode，需填此项）: 全部

**skill名称**（选填，默认取skill name，如有对外展示需求可填）: 

**skill描述**（选填，默认取仓库skill description，如有对外展示需求可填）: 

**作者/组织**（选填，默认为仓库作者/组织，如有对外展示需求可填）: 

## ✨ skill配套实践

**实践标题**（必填）: 

**实践简介**（必填）: 

**实践链接**（必填，如公众号/小红书/抖音/飞书文档/知乎/掘金/B站...等能通过电脑打开的链接）: 

**作者/组织**（选填，默认为实践作者，如有对外展示需求可填）: 


## 🧪 自查清单

- [ ] 我亲自测试过这个 skill
- [ ] skill 能正常工作，功能符合描述
- [ ] 与主流平台（Claude/Codex/Cursor/TRAE）兼容良好
- [ ] 实践符合要求

## 📝 补充说明

其他需要说明的信息（选填）：

---

**skill-cn最大的特色，就是每一个skill，都有配套实践**
**实践主旨：** 讲清该skill，如何解决当前场景问题

> ❌ 只推荐，无场景（如：推荐几个好用skill库）
> ❌ 无落地成果展示（如：skill-creator可以创建skill，完，无实践）
> ❌ 场景发散（如：我用10个skill，解决了10个场景的问题）

> ✅ 面向具体场景提供解决方案（如：如何用pptx + front-design skill 打造炫酷ppt；我用skill-creator，做了一个解决xx问题的skill；我如何利用这几个skill，解决我日常的自媒体写作问题）

**注意事项**：
- 我们只收录经过精心筛选的高质量 skill 服务
- 请确保推荐的 skill，具有实际价值，和良好的维护状态
- 推荐后，我们会在3个工作日内评估完毕。符合标准的skill与实践，会被收录；不符合标准，我们会在issue内进行回复
- 由衷感谢您为 skill 生态做出的贡献！ 🙏

items.content:
# 财经资讯汇总

**最后更新: 2026-04-12 06:33:46**

## 来源: metwarn

[暴雨速報] 天文台在上午11時正發出黃色暴雨警告信號。
表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

位於珠江口的強雷雨區正繼續向東北偏東移動，其強度有所發展，即將開始影響本港。西部地區將首當其衝。讀者應提防大驟雨、猛烈陣風及雷暴。
身在戶外空曠地方的讀者應留意雷暴風險，如有需要應到有遮蔽的地方暫避。

---

本港西南面再有雷雨區正向東北方向移動，預料將在短期內影響市區及港島一帶。讀者應繼續提防大驟雨及雷暴。
身處該區的讀者應持續留意天氣變化，如有需要應到有遮蔽的地方暫避。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午1時40分取消黃色暴雨警告信號。
雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

---

位於香港以南海域的強雷雨區正逐漸向東北移近本港，未來數小時本港部分地區可能受大驟雨、雷暴影響，南部地區將會首當其衝。
身在戶外空曠地方的讀者應留意天氣變化。

---

過去數小時，珠江口一帶海域持續有強雷雨區發展。未來一兩小時香港南部及市區部分地區或再受大驟雨、強陣風及雷暴影響。讀者應提高警覺。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午2時05分發出黃色暴雨警告信號。
表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午5時40分取消黃色暴雨警告信號。
雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

---

中學文憑試筆試將於明日（星期三）開考，主科部分則於星期四開始，MET WARN預先祝福各位考生順順利利，考獲自己心目中的理想成績！
再次提醒各位考生，根據現行準則，即使天文台發出紅色暴雨警告，只要考評局未有宣佈，當日所有考試將如常舉行。考評局相關指引同時提及，若紅色暴雨警告於考試時間前生效，試場主任可彈性處理開考時間，一般會先延遲約15分鐘開考，並可因應需要再延遲15分鐘。
按照現時預測，未來九日本港出現惡劣或不穩定天氣的機會不大。惟明日受到偏東氣流影響，或會有一兩陣驟雨。無論如何，正所謂天有不測之風雲，各位考生出門前都應該留意天氣和交通狀況，免失預算。

---

隨著太平洋高壓增強，並在未來數日支配本港，預料炎熱及晴朗的天氣會持續一段時間。
有異於一般四月偶有東北季候風南下，並夾雜天陰、下雨的天氣，今年四月本港可謂「提早入夏」。從天文台氣溫概率預報亦可見，未來十數日大部分時間，本港最高氣溫均較有可能高於氣候平均。
另一方面，西北太平洋有一個新的熱帶氣旋發展。雖然森拉克吸引了部分媒體的目光，但預料風暴不會進入南海，亦不會直接影響本港天氣。

---

## 来源: kingkitay

【4连板华远控股：本次重组可能涉及控股股东华远集团 不涉及本公司的重大资产重组事项】

---

美联储戴利表示，将通胀率降至2%至关重要，高通胀数据不会让任何人感到意外。

---

交易员上调对美联储年内降息一次的押注。

---

国内商品期货夜盘开盘涨跌不一，合成橡胶跌3.7%，乙二醇跌超2%，甲醇跌1.87%，对二甲苯跌1.83%，燃油跌1.56%，沪铜涨0.93%，焦煤涨0.66%，纯碱涨0.61%。

---

“美联储传声筒”Nick Timiraos表示，美国3月核心CPI略低于预期。不过，单个月份的数据说明不了太多问题。美联储希望看到能源价格回落（并且对关税传导效应已经结束有更多信心），而且能源的连锁影响（如机票、航运价格）更有可能还在后面。
国内化工期货夜盘走低，合成橡胶跌5.5%，乙二醇跌超3%，燃油跌2.6%，甲醇跌超2.5%，丙烯跌逾2%，液化气、苯乙烯跌2%。

---

白宫经济顾问哈塞特表示，美联储仍有降息空间的前景将非常稳固。

items.author: github-actions[bot]

items.title: feat(feed_detail): pushState SPA navigation to bypass captcha
items.content:
## What

Rewrote `GetFeedDetailWithConfig()` to use SPA route navigation instead of direct page navigation, bypassing Xiaohongshu's captcha/security wall.

## Why

Xiaohongshu started redirecting direct `/explore/<noteID>` navigations to a captcha page (`verifyType=124, verifyBiz=461`). This broke all feed detail scraping.

## How

1. Navigate to `/explore` homepage first (doesn't trigger captcha)
2. Inject XHR/fetch interceptors to capture internal API responses
3. Use `history.pushState()` + `dispatchEvent(popstate)` to trigger SPA route change
4. XHS frontend JS handles the route change, loads note data into `__INITIAL_STATE__`
5. Extract data from intercepted XHR or `__INITIAL_STATE__` fallback

## Key Insight

Direct navigation = server-side request = captcha redirect.
`pushState` = client-side only = no new request = no captcha.

## Changes

- `feed_detail.go`: +526 lines
  - `GetFeedDetailWithConfig()` — full rewrite with explore preload + pushState
  - `parseInternalAPIResponse()` — parse intercepted XHR data
  - `extractFromInitialState()` — extract from `__INITIAL_STATE__`
  - `extractFromDOM()` — DOM fallback extraction
  - `checkPageAccessible()` — captcha URL detection

## Testing

Successfully scraped **570/570** notes from a profile with this approach. Rate limit: 5s between requests to avoid triggering captcha on the homepage.

---

items.title: 新增群聊消息发送者识别与成员管理功能
items.content: 啊~ 好累， 思考了很久，实现了很久，也测试了很久。 给群聊监听功能添加上识别发送者的昵称和微信id，以便能否区分不同的消息是谁发送的。 初衷是这样的，今天我把微信群聊监听机制接入自己的龙虾，那么问题来了，虽然可以自动回复群聊，但是其实就是单纯的使用配置的大模型的。然后呢，我就把每个微信群的聊天记录接入我的记忆系统，让微信监听到某位群友进行自动回复的时候可以先扫描之前的记忆，特别是他自己说过的话，这样可以更好的更有针对性的回复他（调侃他），会觉得很好玩，而且呢，这套系统也能够越用越让我的智能体更懂我的每个群，乃至于每一位群友，让它更像是我的数字分身，让它更了解我身边的人。 但是，问题来了，目前微信4.x在当前机制下，收到消息时无法获取到发送者是谁，那还谈何分类记忆，如何记忆哪句话是谁说的？ 如何让它下次回复这位群友的时候搜索这位群友以前说过的话？所以就有了这次的提交。 效果实图：（初步实现肯定还有没发现的BUG，希望这一步能够抛砖引玉吧） 本次运行需要把requirements.txt新增的包安装上。 环境问题解决后直接运行listen_with_sender_info.py就可以测试。 用小号发消息： 用当前登录的账号发消息（会声明是 “自己”，且不需要记录自己的微信id）： 啊，好困~ 晚安了。

items.content:
# 财经资讯汇总

**最后更新: 2026-04-11 19:31:04**

## 来源: metwarn

[暴雨速報] 天文台在上午11時正發出黃色暴雨警告信號。
表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

位於珠江口的強雷雨區正繼續向東北偏東移動，其強度有所發展，即將開始影響本港。西部地區將首當其衝。讀者應提防大驟雨、猛烈陣風及雷暴。
身在戶外空曠地方的讀者應留意雷暴風險，如有需要應到有遮蔽的地方暫避。

---

本港西南面再有雷雨區正向東北方向移動，預料將在短期內影響市區及港島一帶。讀者應繼續提防大驟雨及雷暴。
身處該區的讀者應持續留意天氣變化，如有需要應到有遮蔽的地方暫避。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午1時40分取消黃色暴雨警告信號。
雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

---

位於香港以南海域的強雷雨區正逐漸向東北移近本港，未來數小時本港部分地區可能受大驟雨、雷暴影響，南部地區將會首當其衝。
身在戶外空曠地方的讀者應留意天氣變化。

---

過去數小時，珠江口一帶海域持續有強雷雨區發展。未來一兩小時香港南部及市區部分地區或再受大驟雨、強陣風及雷暴影響。讀者應提高警覺。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午2時05分發出黃色暴雨警告信號。
表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午5時40分取消黃色暴雨警告信號。
雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

---

中學文憑試筆試將於明日（星期三）開考，主科部分則於星期四開始，MET WARN預先祝福各位考生順順利利，考獲自己心目中的理想成績！
再次提醒各位考生，根據現行準則，即使天文台發出紅色暴雨警告，只要考評局未有宣佈，當日所有考試將如常舉行。考評局相關指引同時提及，若紅色暴雨警告於考試時間前生效，試場主任可彈性處理開考時間，一般會先延遲約15分鐘開考，並可因應需要再延遲15分鐘。
按照現時預測，未來九日本港出現惡劣或不穩定天氣的機會不大。惟明日受到偏東氣流影響，或會有一兩陣驟雨。無論如何，正所謂天有不測之風雲，各位考生出門前都應該留意天氣和交通狀況，免失預算。

---

隨著太平洋高壓增強，並在未來數日支配本港，預料炎熱及晴朗的天氣會持續一段時間。
有異於一般四月偶有東北季候風南下，並夾雜天陰、下雨的天氣，今年四月本港可謂「提早入夏」。從天文台氣溫概率預報亦可見，未來十數日大部分時間，本港最高氣溫均較有可能高於氣候平均。
另一方面，西北太平洋有一個新的熱帶氣旋發展。雖然森拉克吸引了部分媒體的目光，但預料風暴不會進入南海，亦不會直接影響本港天氣。

---

過去數小時，珠江口一帶海域持續有強雷雨區發展。未來一兩小時香港南部及市區部分地區或再受大驟雨、強陣風及雷暴影響。讀者應提高警覺。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午2時05分發出黃色暴雨警告信號。
表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午5時40分取消黃色暴雨警告信號。
雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

---

中學文憑試筆試將於明日（星期三）開考，主科部分則於星期四開始，MET WARN預先祝福各位考生順順利利，考獲自己心目中的理想成績！
再次提醒各位考生，根據現行準則，即使天文台發出紅色暴雨警告，只要考評局未有宣佈，當日所有考試將如常舉行。考評局相關指引同時提及，若紅色暴雨警告於考試時間前生效，試場主任可彈性處理開考時間，一般會先延遲約15分鐘開考，並可因應需要再延遲15分鐘。
按照現時預測，未來九日本港出現惡劣或不穩定天氣的機會不大。惟明日受到偏東氣流影響，或會有一兩陣驟雨。無論如何，正所謂天有不測之風雲，各位考生出門前都應該留意天氣和交通狀況，免失預算。

---

隨著太平洋高壓增強，並在未來數日支配本港，預料炎熱及晴朗的天氣會持續一段時間。
有異於一般四月偶有東北季候風南下，並夾雜天陰、下雨的天氣，今年四月本港可謂「提早入夏」。從天文台氣溫概率預報亦可見，未來十數日大部分時間，本港最高氣溫均較有可能高於氣候平均。
另一方面，西北太平洋有一個新的熱帶氣旋發展。雖然森拉克吸引了部分媒體的目光，但預料風暴不會進入南海，亦不會直接影響本港天氣。

---

## 来源: kingkitay

【4连板华远控股：本次重组可能涉及控股股东华远集团 不涉及本公司的重大资产重组事项】
华远控股(
600743.SH
)公告称，公司股票连续3个交易日收盘价格涨幅偏离值累计达20%，4月10日继续涨停，连续4个交易日累计涨幅达46.41%。公司于4月10日披露《关于公司实际控制人筹划重组的提示性公告》。公司控股股东北京市华远集团有限公司（简称“华远集团”）来函告知，本公司实际控制人北京市西城区国资委拟对下属部分一级企业集团进行重组（简称“本次重组”），本次重组可能涉及本公司控股股东华远集团，不涉及本公司的重大资产重组事项，不会导致本公司实际控制人发生变更。截至本公告披露日，相关事项仍处于筹划阶段，尚存在不确定性。

---

美联储戴利描述了降息或按兵不动的几种可能情况，并表示加息的可能性不大。
【美联储戴利：将通胀率降至2%至关重要】
美联储戴利表示，将通胀率降至2%至关重要，高通胀数据不会让任何人感到意外。

---

交易员上调对美联储年内降息一次的押注。

---

国内商品期货夜盘开盘涨跌不一，合成橡胶跌3.7%，乙二醇跌超2%，甲醇跌1.87%，对二甲苯跌1.83%，燃油跌1.56%，沪铜涨0.93%，焦煤涨0.66%，纯碱涨0.61%。

---

【“美联储传声筒”：美国3月核心CPI略低于预期 能源的连锁影响更有可能还在后面】
“美联储传声筒”Nick Timiraos表示，美国3月核心CPI略低于预期。不过，单个月份的数据说明不了太多问题。美联储希望看到能源价格回落（并且对关税传导效应已经结束有更多信心），而且能源的连锁影响（如机票、航运价格）更有可能还在后面。
国内化工期货夜盘走低，合成橡胶跌5.5%，乙二醇跌超3%，燃油跌2.6%，甲醇跌超2.5%，丙烯跌逾2%，液化气、苯乙烯跌2%。

---

白宫经济顾问哈塞特表示，美联储仍有降息空间的前景将非常稳固。
【美股三

# 财经资讯汇总

[暴雨速報] 天文台在上午11時正發出黃色暴雨警告信號。
表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

位於珠江口的強雷雨區正繼續向東北偏東移動，其強度有所發展，即將開始影響本港。西部地區將首當其衝。讀者應提防大驟雨、猛烈陣風及雷暴。
身在戶外空曠地方的讀者應留意雷暴風險，如有需要應到有遮蔽的地方暫避。

---

本港西南面再有雷雨區正向東北方向移動，預料將在短期內影響市區及港島一帶。讀者應繼續提防大驟雨及雷暴。
身處該區的讀者應持續留意天氣變化，如有需要應到有遮蔽的地方暫避。

雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

---

位於香港以南海域的強雷雨區正逐漸向東北移近本港，未來數小時本港部分地區可能受大驟雨、雷暴影響，南部地區將會首當其衝。

表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

再次提醒各位考生，根據現行準則，即使天文台發出紅色暴雨警告，只要考評局未有宣佈，當日所有考試將如常舉行。考評局相關指引同時提及，若紅色暴雨警告於考試時間前生效，試場主任可彈性處理開考時間，一般會先延遲約15分鐘開考，並可因應需要再延遲15分鐘。
按照現時預測，未來九日本港出現惡劣或不穩定天氣的機會不大。惟明日受到偏東氣流影響，或會有一兩陣驟雨。無論如何，正所謂天有不測之風雲，各位考生出門前都應該留意天氣和交通狀況，免失預算。

---

隨著太平洋高壓增強，並在未來數日支配本港，預料炎熱及晴朗的天氣會持續一段時間。
有異於一般四月偶有東北季候風南下，並夾雜天陰、下雨的天氣，今年四月本港可謂「提早入夏」。從天文台氣溫概率預報亦可見，未來十數日大部分時間，本港最高氣溫均較有可能高於氣候平均。
另一方面，西北太平洋有一個新的熱帶氣旋發展。雖然森拉克吸引了部分媒體的目光，但預料風暴不會進入南海，亦不會直接影響本港天氣。

---

## 来源: kingkitay

【4连板华远控股：本次重组可能涉及控股股东华远集团 不涉及本公司的重大资产重组事项】
华远控股(
600743.SH
)公告称，公司股票连续3个交易日收盘价格涨幅偏离值累计达20%，4月10日继续涨停，连续4个交易日累计涨幅达46.41%。公司于4月10日披露《关于公司实际控制人筹划重组的提示性公告》。公司控股股东北京市华远集团有限公司（简称“华远集团”）来函告知，本公司实际控制人北京市西城区国资委拟对下属部分一级企业集团进行重组（简称“本次重组”），本次重组可能涉及本公司控股股东华远集团，不涉及本公司的重大资产重组事项，不会导致本公司实际控制人发生变更。截至本公告披露日，相关事项仍处于筹划阶段，尚存在不确定性。

---

美联储戴利描述了降息或按兵不动的几种可能情况，并表示加息的可能性不大。
【美联储戴利：将通胀率降至2%至关重要】
美联储戴利表示，将通胀率降至2%至关重要，高通胀数据不会让任何人感到意外。

---

交易员上调对美联储年内降息一次的押注。

---

国内商品期货夜盘开盘涨跌不一，合成橡胶跌3.7%，乙二醇跌超2%，甲醇跌1.87%，对二甲苯跌1.83%，燃油跌1.56%，沪铜涨0.93%，焦煤涨0.66%，纯碱涨0.61%。

---

【“美联储传声筒”：美国3月核心CPI略低于预期 能源的连锁影响更有可能还在后面】
“美联储传声筒”Nick Timiraos表示，美国3月核心CPI略低于预期。不过，单个月份的数据说明不了太多问题。美联储希望看到能源价格回落（并且对关税传导效应已经结束有更多信心），而且能源的连锁影响（如机票、航运价格）更有可能还在后面。
国内化工期货夜盘走低，合成橡胶跌5.5%，乙二醇跌超3%，燃油跌2.6%，甲醇跌超2.5%，丙烯跌逾2%，液化气、苯乙烯跌2%。

---

白宫经济顾问哈塞特表示，美联储仍有降息空间的前景将非常稳固。
【美股三

表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

位於珠江口的強雷雨區正繼續向東北偏東移動，其強度有所發展，即將開始影響本港。西部地區將首當其衝。讀者應提防大驟雨、猛烈陣風及雷暴。
身在戶外空曠地方的讀者應留意雷暴風險，如有需要應到有遮蔽的地方暫避。

---

本港西南面再有雷雨區正向東北方向移動，預料將在短期內影響市區及港島一帶。讀者應繼續提防大驟雨及雷暴。

身處該區的讀者應持續留意天氣變化，如有需要應到有遮蔽的地方暫避。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午1時40分取消黃色暴雨警告信號。
雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

---

位於香港以南海域的強雷雨區正逐漸向東北移近本港，未來數小時本港部分地區可能受大驟雨、雷暴影響，南部地區將會首當其衝。
身在戶外空曠地方的讀者應留意天氣變化。

---

過去數小時，珠江口一帶海域持續有強雷雨區發展。未來一兩小時香港南部及市區部分地區或再受大驟雨、強陣風及雷暴影響。讀者應提高警覺。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午2時05分發出黃色暴雨警告信號。
表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午5時40分取消黃色暴雨警告信號。
雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

---

中學文憑試筆試將於明日（星期三）開考，主科部分則於星期四開始，MET WARN預先祝福各位考生順順利利，考獲自己心目中的理想成績！
再次提醒各位考生，根據現行準則，即使天文台發出紅色暴雨警告，只要考評局未有宣佈，當日所有考試將如常舉行。考評局相關指引同時提及，若紅色暴雨警告於考試時間前生效，試場主任可彈性處理開考時間，一般會先延遲約15分鐘開考，並可因應需要再延遲15分鐘。
按照現時預測，未來九日本港出現惡劣或不穩定天氣的機會不大。惟明日受到偏東氣流影響，或會有一兩陣驟雨。無論如何，正所謂天有不測之風雲，各位考生出門前都應該留意天氣和交通狀況，免失預算。

---

隨著太平洋高壓增強，並在未來數日支配本港，預料炎熱及晴朗的天氣會持續一段時間。
有異於一般四月偶有東北季候風南下，並夾雜天陰、下雨的天氣，今年四月本港可謂「提早入夏」。從天文台氣溫概率預報亦可見，未來十數日大部分時間，本港最高氣溫均較有可能高於氣候平均。
另一方面，西北太平洋有一個新的熱帶氣旋發展。雖然森拉克吸引了部分媒體的目光，但預料風暴不會進入南海，亦不會直接影響本港天氣。

---

items.title: ✨ [全端] 在线音视频会议功能 — 人类与 AI 共同参会 + 录制 + 纪要
items.content:
## 反馈人

滕斌（内部员工）

## 场景

团队周会等协作场景，需要 AI（龙虾）作为真正的参会者加入音视频会议，实时旁听并记录。

## 问题/诉求（原话）

> 开发一个在线会议功能，用户和AI机器人都可以参加，可以进行会议录制, 会议纪要

## 期望效果

1. **音视频会议**：Octo 内建在线音视频会议能力（非对接第三方，因腾讯会议/飞书会议不支持第三方 AI 作为参会者接入）
2. **参与者**：人类用户和 AI 龙虾均可作为参会者加入，不区分角色
3. **会议录制**：支持音视频录制 + 回放
4. **会议纪要**：AI 实时语音转文字 + 自动生成会议纪要

## 初步方案

此需求是一个大型独立功能模块，与已有的文字会议群规划（pool#87 会议群自动创建、pool#88 会后纪要生成）不同，这里涉及完整的音视频通信能力。核心能力拆解：

1. **音视频引擎**：需要 WebRTC 或类似方案，支持多人实时音视频通信
2. **AI 参会接入**：龙虾需要能接收音频流进行实时语音识别（ASR），具备"听"的能力
3. **录制服务**：服务端录制 + 存储 + 回放
4. **纪要生成**：基于实时/离线语音转文字，结合 LLM 生成结构化会议纪要

调研发现：腾讯会议有开放 API/SDK 但不支持第三方 AI 作为参会者加入音视频流；飞书会议同理。因此建议 Octo 自建音视频会议能力，或评估是否有其他可嵌入的开源方案（如 Jitsi、LiveKit 等）。

## 原始来源

DMWork group:Bug反馈群 user:滕斌 (uid:8c56bed1340749949e6fb1d1b5836d33)

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

# OpenClaw 项目动态日报 (2026-04-12)

## 1. 今日速览
OpenClaw 项目今日活跃度极高，共处理了 **500 条 Issues** 和 **500 条 PRs** 更新，其中新开/活跃 Issues 达 444 条，显示出社区反馈极其热烈。项目发布了 **v2026.4.11** 正式版，重点增强了 "Dreaming" 记忆系统的 ChatGPT 数据导入能力。虽然新版本发布带来了功能迭代，但 PR 积压量较大（待合并 337 条），且出现多个关于 OAuth 认证和新模型支持的回归问题，项目正处于功能快速演进与稳定性维护的博弈期。

## 2. 版本发布
**版本号**: v2026.4.11  
**发布时间**: 2026-04-12  
**更新重点**:
- **Dreaming/memory-wiki 增强**: 新增 ChatGPT 对话记录导入功能，UI 界面新增 `Imported Insights` 和 `Memory Palace` 日记子标签，用户现可直接在 UI 中检查导入的源对话、编译的 Wiki 页面及完整源页面 ([#64505](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/64505))。
- **Control UI/webchat**: 改进了助手媒体内容的渲染逻辑。

**Beta 版本**: v2026.4.11-beta.1 (内容同上)。

## 3. 项目进展

items.title: Backport shared WhatsApp / channel orchestration primitives from downstream enterprise fork
items.content:
# Background
The downstream enterprise fork has validated a `whatsapp-agent` orchestration layer for customer-service style message handling. Some parts are business-specific and must remain downstream, but several pieces are reusable channel primitives that belong in the AAS base.

# Backport scope
The following should be abstracted and backported into AAS base as channel-agnostic capability rather than furniture-specific logic:
- message state machine for inbound channel handling
- conversation store / recent history interface
- manual handoff gate
- business-hours gate
- low-confidence fallback gate
- group activation gate
- webhook / bridge ingress contract for runtime channels such as OpenClaw WhatsApp

# Must stay downstream
- owner vs customer business role semantics
- furniture-industry prompts and safety boundaries
- CRM field mapping
- enterprise fallback copy
- private KB and lead workflow integration

# Expected outcome
- extract the generic abstractions into AAS base
- keep downstream adapters thin
- avoid copying vertical business logic into upstream

# Downstream reference
- autonomous-agent-stack/furniture-enterprise-ai-platform issue #3

items.title: Cron job --model override is ignored during execution
items.content:
**Version:** 2026.3.28 (f9b1079)

**Summary:** When creating or editing a cron job with --model, the model is correctly stored in the job configuration (visible in openclaw cron list and ~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json), but the actual execution always uses the default agent model instead.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Create a cron job with explicit model: openclaw cron add --name test --at 1m --session isolated --message test --model PROVIDER/MODEL --announce --channel discord --to channel:ID --delete-after-run
2. Verify config: openclaw cron list shows the configured model
3. Verify payload: ~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json contains the model in the payload
4. Trigger execution: openclaw cron run ID
5. Check result: openclaw cron runs --id ID shows the default model (not the configured one)

**Expected:** Job executes with the configured model

**Actual:** Job executes with the default agent model, ignoring the per-job configuration

**Additional notes:**
- Model override is stored correctly but never applied during execution
- Appears to affect all cron job types (one-shot and recurring)
items.author: Dorapower

items.title: feat(daemon): add Google Gemini CLI backend
items.content:
## Summary

Registers `gemini` (Google's [Gemini CLI](https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli)) as a sixth supported agent provider alongside `claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, `openclaw`, and `hermes`.

## Motivation

I'm running a self-hosted Multica instance on a machine where Gemini CLI is already installed and authenticated (alongside Claude Code). Hard-coding the probe list in `daemon/config.go` to the existing five providers was the only thing keeping Gemini off the supported list — the rest of the architecture (polymorphic `Backend` interface, pluggable agent providers, execenv meta-skill injection) is already generic.

## Change

- **Daemon config probe** (`server/internal/daemon/config.go`) — adds a `gemini` entry to the auto-detection loop, using `MULTICA_GEMINI_PATH` / `MULTICA_GEMINI_MODEL` env overrides to mirror the other providers. If `gemini` is not on PATH, the provider is simply absent at runtime — no behavior change for existing deployments.
- **New backend** (`server/pkg/agent/gemini.go`) — `geminiBackend` implements the `Backend` interface by spawning:
  ```
  gemini -p <prompt> --yolo -o text [-m <model>] [-r <session>]
  ```
  and collecting stdout to completion. Returns a single `MessageText` on the message channel plus a `Result` with `Status` / `Output` / `DurationMs`. Context deadline is translated to `\"timeout\"` status to match the existing Claude/Codex contracts.
- **Agent factory** (`server/pkg/agent/agent.go`) — adds the `gemini` case to `New()` and updates the supported-types list in the error message and doc comment.
- **Runtime config injection** (`server/internal/daemon/execenv/runtime_config.go`) — adds a `gemini` case that writes a `GEMINI.md` file into the task workdir, mirroring the existing `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` injection for the other providers. Gemini CLI discovers `GEMINI.md` natively, so the meta-skill content (available `multica` commands, agent identity, workflow guidance) flows through without any Gemini-specific

items.title: feat(daemon): per-agent MCP support via runtime_config.mcp_servers
items.content:
## Problem

The daemon spawns Claude with `--strict-mcp-config` but never pairs it with `--mcp-config`. In Claude Code semantics, `--strict-mcp-config` means *"use only MCP servers from the `--mcp-config` flag, ignoring user/project/local settings"* — so without a file, **zero MCP servers are available to any agent**.

I discovered this while testing a self-hosted Multica instance: assigning an issue to an agent with instructions like *"use the filesystem MCP to list /tmp"* consistently failed because the agent's tool set never contained `mcp__fs__*`. The agent correctly reported the tool was missing and marked the issue blocked — it wasn't a prompt/instructions problem, it was platform-level MCP isolation.

The existing `agents.runtime_config` JSONB column is already in the schema but unused by the daemon, making this a clean place to add per-agent MCP configuration.

## Change

An agent's `runtime_config` JSONB may now contain an `mcp_servers` object in the same shape as Claude Code's native `mcpServers` map:

```json
{
  "mcp_servers": {
    "fs":    {"command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/tmp"]},
    "fetch": {"command": "uvx", "args": ["mcp-server-fetch"]}
  }
}
```

End-to-end flow:

1. **Server** — `ClaimTaskByRuntime` extracts `mcp_servers` from `agent.runtime_config` and returns it as `TaskAgentData.MCPServers` (`json.RawMessage`). Extraction helper (`extractMCPServers`) is defensive: nil / invalid JSON / missing key → nil, never an error.
2. **Daemon** — `AgentData.MCPServers` mirrors the field and flows into `execenv.TaskContextForEnv.MCPServers`.
3. **Execenv** — `writeMCPConfig` writes `{workdir}/.mcp.json` wrapped as `{"mcpServers": <raw>}` and records the path on `Environment.MCPConfigPath`. Only writes for `provider == "claude"` — a no-op for every other backend. Empty / null / `{}` / non-object all short-circuit to "no file written."
4. **Daemon** — passes `env.MCPConfigPath` as `ExecOptions.MCPConfigPath` to t

items.title: fix: preserve anthropic replay safety for signed thinking sessions
items.content:
## Summary
- preserve Anthropic signed-thinking turns only when sibling tool calls are already replay-safe
- drop signed-thinking assistant turns that would require tool-call mutation, stale allowlist rewrites, or attachment redaction
- cover the poisoned-session replay path and the new unsafe-turn fallback behavior with targeted regression tests

## Why
Historical Anthropic assistant turns that contain signed `thinking` / `redacted_thinking` blocks cannot be mutated during replay. The initial fix preserved too much and skipped other hygiene work. This version keeps replay-safe signed turns intact but drops unsafe ones before they can leak inline `sessions_spawn` attachment payloads or forward malformed / stale tool calls.

items.title: Appeal: Platform banner contradicts OpenClaw's own Benign verdict — Hardcore v5.1.0
items.content:
Hi ClawHub team,

I'd like to appeal the "Suspicious" platform banner on `/ai-control-protocol-hardcore` (v5.1.0).

Here's why I think this flag warrants a second look:

**Your own systems disagree with each other.**

OpenClaw contextual analysis: **Benign — HIGH CONFIDENCE**
> *"The skill's instructions match its name and description and request no extra privileges or secrets."
*

Static analysis flag: `SKILL.MD:1` — Skill is configured with `always=true` (persistent invocation)

The static analysis note on the same page reads:
> *"These patterns were detected by automated regex scanning. They may be normal for skills that integrate with external APIs."
*

So the flag was triggered by a regex match on `always=true`, while your own contextual analyzer — with high confidence — cleared it. The platform-level red banner appears to be driven by the regex result alone, overriding the contextual verdict.

**Why `always=true` is correct behavior here**

This skill is a behavioral constraint layer, not a tool or API integration. Its job is to enforce cognitive integrity rules (no flattery, no unverified claims presented as facts, no logical gaps papered over) across every agent session. A constraint that only activates sometimes is not a constraint.

There are no credentials requested, no external calls, no code executed. The "always active" flag is the entire point of the skill.

**My ask**

Given that OpenClaw's contextual analysis returned Benign at HIGH CONFIDENCE, would it be possible to let the contextual result take precedence over the static regex in this case? Or alternatively, is there a specific field in the skill manifest where I can annotate the intent of `always=true` to prevent the regex trigger?

Happy to provide the full skill source for review. Thanks.
items.author: DaibinThink

items.title: fix: preserve explicit Discord DM targets in isolated cron delivery
items.content:
## Summary
- preserve `resolvedDelivery.to` and `threadId` when handing isolated cron runs into `executeCronRun(...)`
- preserve explicit Discord `user:<id>` targets right before `dispatchCronDelivery(...)`

## Problem
Isolated cron and hook-driven runs could lose the explicit Discord DM target during delivery prep. In practice, a configured DM target like `user:105779346605658112` could degrade into `channel:105779346605658112`, causing Discord delivery failures such as `Unknown Channel`.

## Root cause
There were two linked issues in `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts`:
1. the `resolvedDelivery` object passed into `executeCronRun(...)` only kept `channel` and `accountId`, dropping `to` and `threadId`
2. by the time final delivery was dispatched, the explicit DM target was no longer guaranteed to survive the isolated-run handoff

## Fix
- include `to` and `threadId` in the `resolvedDelivery` object passed to `executeCronRun(...)`
- add a narrow preservation guard before `dispatchCronDelivery(...)` that restores the original explicit Discord `user:<id>` target from job delivery config when present

## Validation
Local validation against an installed OpenClaw build showed the delivery trace changed from:
- `to=channel:105779346605658112`

to:
- `to=user:105779346605658112`
- `deliverViaDirect results=1 hadPartialFailure=false`

This was verified using `/hooks/agent` with:
- `channel: "discord"`
- `to: "user:105779346605658112"`
- `deliver: true`

## Notes
This is intentionally narrow to Discord explicit DM targets in the isolated cron/hook delivery path, to avoid broad target-resolution behavior changes.

items.content:
## Summary

`sessions_spawn` calls with `runtime="acp"` and `agentId="cursor"` fail reliably on openclaw 2026.4.5 with one of two errors:

- `ACP_BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE`: "ACP runtime backend is currently unavailable. Try again in a moment."
- `ACP_BACKEND_MISSING`: "ACP runtime backend is not configured. Install and enable the acpx runtime plugin."

Even though `[plugins] embedded acpx runtime backend registered` appears in the gateway log at startup and the acpx plugin is correctly enabled in `plugins.allow` + `plugins.entries`.

## Environment

- openclaw 2026.4.5 (3e72c03)
- macOS 15.4 / Darwin 25.4.0
- Node 22
- Cursor ACP stack: `acpx 0.5.3`, `cursor-agent` installed, `~/.acpx/sessions/` has 60+ prior sessions (so ACP *has* worked in the past)

## Reproduction

1. Fresh gateway start (`launchctl kickstart -k gui/<uid>/ai.openclaw.gateway`)
2. Verify gateway log shows: `[plugins] embedded acpx runtime backend registered (cwd: ...)`

     runtime: "acp",

items.title: feat: add Hermes Agent as native spawn target
items.content:
## Summary

Adds first-class support for [Hermes Agent](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent) CLI as a spawn target in ClawTeam, alongside existing agents (OpenClaw, Claude, Codex, Gemini, Kimi, nanobot, Qwen, OpenCode).

After this merges: `clawteam spawn hermes --team my-team --agent-name researcher --task "..."` works end-to-end on both tmux and subprocess backends. Each spawned Hermes gets a tool-tagged session (so it doesn't pollute the user's session list), gbrain MCP access (since the parent's MCP config is inherited), and full worker isolation via git worktree + tmux window.

## What changed

**Core adapter support** (3 files):
- `clawteam/spawn/command_validation.py` -- `is_hermes_command()` detector, included in `is_interactive_cli()`
- `clawteam/spawn/adapters.py` -- Hermes case in `NativeCliAdapter.prepare_command()` (chat insertion, --source tool, --yolo, -q prompt)
- `clawteam/spawn/tmux_backend.py` -- Hermes case in `TmuxBackend.spawn()` (same flags + -m model forwarding)
- `clawteam/spawn/subprocess_backend.py` -- Hermes case in `SubprocessBackend.spawn()` (for Windows and explicit subprocess users)

**Tests** (2 files, 11 new tests):
- `tests/test_adapters.py` -- 9 unit tests: detection, chat insertion, --source tool, --yolo, prompt via -q, no --continue on fresh spawn, preservation of global options, preservation of alternate subcommands
- `tests/test_spawn_backends.py` -- 2 backend tests (tmux and subprocess) verifying the full spawn command shape

**Docs**:
- `README.md` -- Hermes added to the agents badge, prose list, and supported agents table

## How it works

When ClawTeam invokes `hermes`, the adapter builds:

```
hermes chat --yolo --source tool -q "<task>"
```

Key design decisions:

- **`chat` subcommand** inserted only when the user passes bare `hermes` (detected via `len(normalized_command) == 1`). Preserves global options like `--profile` and alternate subcommands like `sessions`, `model`, `setup`.
- **`--source tool`** tags th

# Hacker News
Artemis II safely splashes down ## 阿耳忒弥斯 II 任务成功返回地球：关键事件总结 以下是关于阿耳忒弥斯 II 任务的关键事件总结： **任务概述：** 阿耳忒弥斯 II 任务于 2026 年 4 月 10 日成功完成，标志着美国宇航局 (NASA) 在重返月球探索道路上的重要一步。这艘飞船搭载着四名宇航员绕月飞行，并安全返回地球。 **关键事件时间线：** * **4 月 10 日 19:13 (美国东部时间):** 阿耳忒弥斯 II 飞船“Integrity”在加利福尼亚州圣地亚哥附近太平洋海域成功溅落，完成了 10 天的绕月飞行任务。NASA 称其为“完美的精准溅落”。 * **4 月 10 日 19:34 - 19:44:** 宇航员通过前沿阳台（充气救生筏）从飞船中撤离，海军潜水员协助转移。 * **4 月 10 日 19:44:** 宇航员被成功吊装到等待的海军直升机上，并飞往救援船“约翰·P·墨瑟”号。 * **4 月 10 日 19:52:** 美国前总统特朗普通过社交媒体表达了对宇航员的祝贺，并表示计划在白宫欢迎他们。 * **4 月 10 日 20:02 - 20:10:** NASA 官员在休斯顿的新闻发布会上对任务的成功表示祝贺，并强调这是未来更多月球任务的开端。 * **4 月 10 日 20:26:** NASA 官员称赞了宇航员家属在飞船重返大气层期间表现出的勇气。 * **4 月 10 日 20:39:** 美国宇航局正在排除飞船与救援队伍之间的通讯问题。 * **4 月 10 日 20:47:** NASA 确认宇航员在重返过程中经历了高达 3.9 G 的重力加速度。 * **4 月 10 日 20:58:** 宇航员确认已穿戴好橙色的 Orion 乘员生存系统套装。 * **4 月 10 日 21:35:** 宇航员在距地球 61,326 英里处醒来。 * **4 月 10 日 21:46:** NASA 确认天气状况适宜溅落。 * **4 月 10 日 21:54:** 飞船预计将以每小时 24,000 英里的速度撞击大气层。 * **4 月 10 日 22:00:** 宇航员在绕月飞行期间观看了一次太阳日食，并拍摄了“地球落日”的照片。 **重要人物：** * **Reid Wiseman (指挥官)** * **Victor Glover (驾驶员)** * **Christina Koch (任务专家)** * **Jeremy Hansen (任务专家)** * **Amit Kshatriya (NASA 副社长)** * **Howard Hu (Orion 计划经理)** * **Shawn Quinn (探索系统项目经理)** * **Jared Isaacman (NASA 局长)** * **Donald Trump (美国前总统)** **其他重要信息：** * 宇航员在任务期间创造了人类离地球最远距离的记录，为 252,756 英里。 * 宇航员在飞船舷窗上发现并命名了一个月球陨石坑，以纪念指挥官 Reid Wiseman 已故的妻子 Carroll。 * 任务期间，宇航员使用 iPhone 拍摄了地球和月球的壮丽照片。 * 任务期间，宇航员在飞船上使用了一个由 8 岁加州儿童设计的零重力指示器 "Rise"。 总而言之，阿耳忒弥斯 II 任务的成功为 NASA 的未来月球探索计划奠定了基础，并为人类重返月球铺平了道路。

items.title: Support configuring a remote agent as the CEO agent during initial setup
items.content:
Hi,

I'm trying to use Paperclip with a remote agent exposed through OpenClaw Gateway, but I can't find a way to use that remote agent as the CEO agent during the initial setup.

At the moment, it looks like the CEO agent can only be selected from agents installed on the local machine, such as Codex, Claude Code, or Hermes. That makes sense for a local-first setup, but it seems to leave out cases where the main agent is running remotely behind a gateway.

I went through the docs and the available configuration options expecting to find some way to do this, whether that meant:

* registering a remote agent as a valid CEO agent
* pointing the CEO agent to a remote endpoint
* following a documented setup flow for OpenClaw Gateway or a similar remote agent

I could not find anything that covers this.

What I want is pretty simple: I want to use a remote agent as Paperclip's main CEO agent instead of being limited to agents installed on the local machine.

What I expected:
There would be a way during setup to choose either a local agent or a remote agent reachable through something like OpenClaw Gateway.

What seems to happen instead:
The setup only recognizes locally installed agents, so there is no obvious way to configure a remote CEO agent.

If this is not supported yet, I think it would be a useful feature. Remote agents feel like a reasonable use case, especially if someone wants the main orchestration agent to live outside the local machine.

If this is already possible, a pointer to the relevant docs or an example config would be really helpful, because I wasn't able to find it.

**Related issues/discussions**
https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/discussions/1940

items.title: fix: gracefully handle missing QA scenario pack in npm distributions (closes #65082)
items.content:
Closes #65082.

## Summary
The `Completion cache update` step fails with a fatal error when `qa/scenarios/index.md` is not present in the installed npm package.

```
Error: qa scenario pack not found: qa/scenarios/index.md
```

The `qa/scenarios/` directory is listed in `package.json` "files" but is not always included in the published npm package (possibly due to build/publish timing or sparse checkout issues).

## Fix
Instead of throwing an error when the QA scenario pack is missing, return an empty pack with default agent identity. This allows:
- Completion cache updates to succeed
- Other consumers of `readQaScenarioPack()` to continue working
- QA scenarios to remain unavailable gracefully in npm distributions

## Why This Matters
- Fatal error during package update (`openclaw update`)
- QA scenarios are primarily for internal testing, not end-user functionality
- Simple defensive fix with zero risk to existing functionality
- Users can still use all other OpenClaw features normally

items.title: Bug: dispatch_via_gateway WS auth challenge not handled — tasks stuck in pending
items.content:
# Bug Report: dispatch_via_gateway WS Auth Challenge Not Handled

**Reported:** 2026-04-12
**Severity:** High
**Affects:** webhook-processor.py — `dispatch_via_gateway()`
**Status:** Confirmed

---

## Bug Description

`dispatch_via_gateway()` in `tasks/webhook-processor.py` tries to dispatch tasks to Marty via WebSocket but does NOT handle the gateway's HMAC auth challenge.

When a WS connection is established to `ws://127.0.0.1:18789/ws/discord/{channel_id}?session_id=...&model=...`, the OpenClaw gateway immediately sends a `connect.challenge` event:

```json
{"type":"event","event":"connect.challenge","payload":{"nonce":"534b3e5c-2ae1-4fca-bb6d-9221ed4fec2d","ts":1775961165555}}
```

`dispatch_via_gateway` sends the prompt message BEFORE responding to the challenge. The gateway then returns an HTML error page (not JSON), causing `json.loads()` to throw an exception.

**Result:** Tasks dispatched via the GitHub webhook (issue label events) never reach Marty. The workflow stays in `stage=marty, stage1_status=pending` forever.

---

## Root Cause

In `tasks/webhook-processor.py`, `dispatch_via_gateway()` at line ~110:

```python
async with websockets.connect(ws_url, ping_interval=None, open_timeout=10) as ws:
    await ws.send(json.dumps({"type": "message", "content": prompt, "channel_id": channel_id}))
    # ^^^ Sends message BEFORE handling auth challenge — gateway rejects it
```

The gateway requires a signed challenge response before accepting messages. This is a security feature — the gateway won't process messages from unauthenticated connections.

---

## How Other Code Handles It

The `workflow-engine.py`'s `dispatch_agent_via_gateway()` (line ~166) does NOT use WebSocket directly. Instead it spawns:

```bash
openclaw agent --agent main --session-id <id> --message <prompt> --timeout 30
```

This CLI handles all auth internally and is the correct approach. The `github-issue-monitor.py` → `dispatch.py` path works correctly for this reason.

**The `webhook-proc

items.content:
## 背景

everything-claude-code 新增了 Hermes/OpenClaw → ECC 迁移指南（HERMES-OPENCLAW-MIGRATION.md），ECC 2.0 提供了 `ecc migrate audit` 系列命令来盘点旧工作空间。

## 目标

为 OpenClaw 用户创建一个迁移审计 Skill，自动：
1. 盘点本地 OpenClaw workspace 中的 skills、agents、hooks、cron jobs
2. 分析可迁移到新系统的组件
3. 生成迁移报告

## 思路

```
openclaw-audit/
  SKILL.md
  scripts/
    scan-workspace.sh    # 扫描 ~/Library/pnpm/global/5/.pnpm/openclaw@*/skills/
    analyze-skills.sh    # 分析 skill 可复用性
    generate-report.sh   # 生成 markdown 迁移报告
```

## 子任务

- [ ] 创建 openclaw-audit skill 目录结构
- [ ] 实现 workspace 扫描脚本
- [ ] 实现 skill 可复用性分析
- [ ] 生成迁移报告 markdown
- [ ] 在 Thomas 现有 workspace 上测试

- 来源: everything-claude-code ECC 2.0 迁移框架启发
- 相关: ECC migrate audit 命令设计

items.title: Architectural Evolution to Universal Agent Infrastructure - 2026-07-25
items.content:
This submission represents the daily architectural evolution of the MCP Any infrastructure.

Key highlights:
1. **Market Ingestion**: Captured shifts in OpenClaw (SMH), Claude Code (NBTA), and Gemini CLI (HRP) identifying coordination latency as the primary swarm bottleneck.
2. **Strategic Pivot**: Transitioned toward Optimistic Governance to neutralize the "Tunneling Tax" in multi-node meshes and CRDT-native coordination to resolve teammate stalls.
3. **Design Evolution**: Introduced the Optimistic Attestation Middleware (OAM) design to decouple tool execution from slow-path attestation via hardware-bound temporary session keys.
4. **Engineering Alignment**: Updated the living feature inventory and engineering roadmaps for both Server and UI to prioritize OAM, CRDT Sharding, and Hierarchical Provenance.

All changes adhere to the "No Destructive Overwrite" and "Strict Formatting" directives.

items.title: Layered, not bundled vs browser-use
items.content:
## Summary

Sharpens the browserclaw-agent value proposition around a concrete **driver / vehicle / electricity** metaphor and a **lineage story**: browserclaw is the Playwright MCP snapshot-and-ref approach, refined through OpenClaw into a standalone npm library. This agent rides on that library. browser-use rolled its own monolithic Python stack.

Two new surfaces, same story, on both the README and the landing page:

### 1. "Layered, not bundled" — the architecture

- **⚡ LLM** — the electricity. Your choice: Claude, GPT, Gemini, local.
- **😎 Agent** — the driver. Built specifically for our 🏎️.
- **🏎️ BrowserClaw** — the vehicle. Standalone npm library, drivable by any agent.

Tagline: *"browser-use welds the 😎 driver to the 🏎️ vehicle. We keep them as separate, swappable layers — drive our 🏎️ with your own 😎, put different ⚡ behind our wheel, or run the whole stack as-is."*

The layering is one-directional: our 🏎️ works with any 😎, but our 😎 is purpose-built for our 🏎️. Copy reflects that and does not claim the reverse.

### 2. "vs browser-use" — the sourced comparison

5-row architectural comparison, **every row cited from browser-use's repo at HEAD (v0.12.6)**:

|                                                                |     browserclaw     |               browser-use                |
| -------------------------------------------------------------- | :-----------------: | :--------------------------------------: |
| 🏎️ Browser engine as a standalone library                      | ✓ `npm i browserclaw` |         ✗ (monolithic package)          |
| Use the 🏎️ with your own 😎                                    |    ✓ (documented)   | ~ (exports exist, not a documented path) |
| 📚 Auto-learned skill catalog per domain                       |          ✓          |     ✗ (skills fetched from Cloud API)    |
| 🛡️ Anti-bot solvers in OSS (Turnstile, press-hold, popups)    |          ✓          |    ~ (OSS only waits for Cloud solver)    |
| 📦 TypeScript / Node
